narrative:
Ørjan Nyland is Norway's starting goalkeeper, boasting an overall rating of 7.42/10. This places him firmly among the top 5% of goalkeepers competing at this tournament. His consistent performances have clearly established his high standing.

In 360 minutes played at this World Cup, Nyland averages 2.5 saves per 90 minutes, highlighting his shot-stopping prowess. He also contributes to defensive organization with 2.0 clearances per 90 minutes and 0.25 interceptions per 90 minutes. His expected assists per 90 minutes at this World Cup are 0.003, as expected for a player in his position.

Nyland plays a critical role as Norway's last line of defense, having featured in every minute of their campaign. Scouts and fans should closely monitor his continued ability to make key saves and organize the defense. His performance in upcoming matches will be vital, especially as 88% of his overall rating is derived from club form.
